passage_title: Sheet glass manufacture: the float process

sentences.sentence: Glass, which has been made since the time of the Mesopotamians and Egyptians, is little more than a mixture of sand, soda ash and lime.
sentences.translation: 自美索不达米亚人和埃及人时代起就开始制造的玻璃，不过是沙子、纯碱和石灰的混合物。

structure.text: which has been made since the time of the Mesopotamians and Egyptians
little more than a mixture of sand, soda ash and lime

difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 Glass 的历史。
little more than 表示“不过是、无非是”，带有弱化强调。

sentences.sentence: When heated to about 1500 degrees Celsius (℃) this becomes a molten mass that hardens when slowly cooled.
sentences.translation: 当这种混合物被加热到约1500摄氏度时，会变成熔融物；缓慢冷却时，它会硬化。

structure.text: When heated to about 1500 degrees Celsius (℃)

difficult_points: When heated... 是省略结构，完整意思是 when this is heated。
that 修饰 a molten mass；when slowly cooled 也省略了 it is。

sentences.sentence: The first successful method for making clear, flat glass involved spinning.
sentences.translation: 第一种成功制造透明平板玻璃的方法是旋转法。

structure.text: The first successful method for making clear, flat glass

difficult_points: for making... 是介词短语，修饰 method，说明该方法的用途。
involve 后接动名词 spinning，表示“包含、需要采用”。

sentences.sentence: This method was very effective as the glass had not touched any surfaces between being soft and becoming hard, so it stayed perfectly unblemished, with a 'fire finish'.
sentences.translation: 这种方法非常有效，因为玻璃从柔软到变硬的过程中没有接触任何表面，因此始终完美无瑕，并带有一种“火焰抛光”的表面效果。

structure.text: as the glass had not touched any surfaces between being soft and becoming hard

difficult_points: as 引导原因状语从句；had not touched 用过去完成时强调先前未接触表面。
between 后面并列的是动名词 being 和 becoming；so 引出结果。

sentences.sentence: However, the process took a long time and was labour intensive.
sentences.translation: 然而，这个过程耗时很长，而且劳动密集。
difficult_points: take a long time 是固定表达，表示“耗时很长”。
后半句与 took 并列，共用同一主语 the process。

sentences.sentence: Nevertheless, demand for flat glass was very high and glassmakers across the world were looking for a method of making it continuously.
sentences.translation: 尽管如此，市场对平板玻璃的需求很高，世界各地的玻璃制造商都在寻找一种能连续生产它的方法。

structure.text: glassmakers across the world were looking for a method of making it continuously

difficult_points: demand for... 表示“对……的需求”。
of making it continuously 修饰 method，it 指 flat glass。

sentences.sentence: The first continuous ribbon process involved squeezing molten glass through two hot rollers, similar to an old mangle.
sentences.translation: 第一种连续带状生产工艺是把熔融玻璃从两个热辊之间挤压通过，这两个辊子类似老式轧衣机。

structure.text: squeezing molten glass through two hot rollers

difficult_points: involved 后接动名词短语，说明工艺包含的操作。
similar to... 后置修饰 two hot rollers，而非 molten glass。

sentences.sentence: This allowed glass of virtually any thickness to be made non-stop, but the rollers would leave both sides of the glass marked, and these would then need to be ground and polished.
sentences.translation: 这使得几乎任何厚度的玻璃都能不间断地生产出来，但辊子会在玻璃两面留下痕迹，因此这些表面还需要打磨和抛光。

structure.text: glass of virtually any thickness to be made non-stop
the rollers would leave both sides of the glass marked
these would then need to be ground and polished

difficult_points: allow + 名词 + to be done 是允许某物被做的结构。
leave ... marked 是“使……留下痕迹”；these 指前面的 both sides of the glass。

sentences.sentence: This part of the process rubbed away around 20 per cent of the glass, and the machines were very expensive.
sentences.translation: 这一工序会磨掉大约20%的玻璃，而且机器非常昂贵。

structure.text: around 20 per cent of the glass

difficult_points: rub away 是短语动词，表示通过摩擦磨掉。
and 连接两个完整分句，后句转而说明设备成本。

sentences.sentence: The float process for making flat glass was invented by Alistair Pilkington.
sentences.translation: 制造平板玻璃的浮法工艺由阿利斯泰尔·皮尔金顿发明。

structure.text: The float process for making flat glass

difficult_points: was invented 是被动语态，突出工艺而非发明者。
for making flat glass 修饰 process，说明用途。

sentences.sentence: This process allows the manufacture of clear, tinted and coated glass for buildings, and clear and tinted glass for vehicles.
sentences.translation: 这种工艺可以生产用于建筑的透明、有色和镀膜玻璃，以及用于车辆的透明和有色玻璃。

structure.text: the manufacture of clear, tinted and coated glass for buildings
clear and tinted glass for vehicles

difficult_points: the manufacture of... 表示“对……的制造”，是 allows 的宾语。
两个并列名词短语分别列出建筑与车辆用玻璃。

sentences.sentence: Pilkington had been experimenting with improving the melting process, and in 1952 he had the idea of using a bed of molten metal to form the flat glass, eliminating altogether the need for rollers within the float bath.
sentences.translation: 皮尔金顿一直在试验改进熔化工艺；1952年，他想到用一层熔融金属来成形平板玻璃，从而彻底取消浮锡槽内使用辊子的必要。

structure.text: had been experimenting with improving the melting process
he had the idea of using a bed of molten metal to form the flat glass
eliminating altogether the need for rollers within the float bath

difficult_points: had been experimenting 表示在某个过去时点前一直持续进行的试验。
of using... 修饰 idea；eliminating... 表示这一设想带来的结果。

sentences.sentence: The metal had to melt at a temperature less than the hardening point of glass (about 600°C), but could not boil at a temperature below the temperature of the molten glass (about 1500°C).
sentences.translation: 这种金属的熔点必须低于玻璃的硬化点（约600摄氏度），但沸点又不能低于熔融玻璃的温度（约1500摄氏度）。

structure.text: at a temperature less than the hardening point of glass (about 600°C)
could not boil at a temperature below the temperature of the molten glass (about 1500°C)

difficult_points: had to 表示必须满足的条件，could not 表示另一项限制。
less than 与 below 都表示比较，但分别修饰熔点和沸点。

sentences.sentence: The best metal for the job was tin.
sentences.translation: 最适合这项工作的金属是锡。

structure.text: The best metal for the job

difficult_points: for the job 后置修饰 metal，说明“适合这项工作”。
tin 是表语，直接给出结论。

sentences.sentence: The rest of the concept relied on gravity, which guaranteed that the surface of the molten metal was perfectly flat and horizontal.
sentences.translation: 这一构想的其余部分依靠重力，而重力保证熔融金属表面完全平整且水平。

structure.text: which guaranteed that the surface of the molten metal was perfectly flat and horizontal

difficult_points: which 指代 gravity，补充说明重力的作用。
that 从句作 guaranteed 的宾语，flat 和 horizontal 并列作表语。

sentences.sentence: Consequently, when pouring molten glass onto the molten tin, the underside of the glass would also be perfectly flat.
sentences.translation: 因此，当把熔融玻璃倒在熔融锡上时，玻璃的底面也会完全平整。

structure.text: when pouring molten glass onto the molten tin

difficult_points: when pouring... 是省略结构，逻辑上指在把玻璃倒上去时。
would 表示工艺条件下预期会出现的结果。

sentences.sentence: If the glass were kept hot enough, it would flow over the molten tin until the top surface was also flat, horizontal and perfectly parallel to the bottom surface.
sentences.translation: 如果玻璃能保持足够热，它就会在熔融锡上流动，直到上表面也变得平整、水平，并与底面完全平行。

structure.text: If the glass were kept hot enough
until the top surface was also flat, horizontal and perfectly parallel to the bottom surface

difficult_points: were kept 是虚拟条件句的被动形式，表示假设条件。
until 引导时间界限；flat、horizontal 和 parallel 并列描述上表面。

sentences.sentence: Once the glass cooled to 604°C or less it was too hard to mark and could be transported out of the cooling zone by rollers.
sentences.translation: 玻璃一旦冷却到604摄氏度或更低，就会硬到无法留下痕迹，并可由辊子运出冷却区。

structure.text: Once the glass cooled to 604°C or less
could be transported out of the cooling zone by rollers

difficult_points: Once 引导时间状语从句，表示“一旦”。
too hard to mark 表示“硬到无法被划出痕迹”；could be transported 是被动语态。

sentences.sentence: The glass settled to a thickness of six millimetres because of surface tension interactions between the glass and the tin.
sentences.translation: 由于玻璃和锡之间的表面张力相互作用，玻璃最终稳定在六毫米厚。

structure.text: to a thickness of six millimetres
because of surface tension interactions between the glass and the tin

difficult_points: settle to 表示逐渐稳定到某一数值或状态。
because of 后接名词短语，说明原因，不同于 because 引导从句。

sentences.sentence: By fortunate coincidence, 60 per cent of the flat glass market at that time was for six-millimetre glass.
sentences.translation: 幸运的是，当时平板玻璃市场中有60%正好需要六毫米玻璃。

structure.text: 60 per cent of the flat glass market at that time

difficult_points: By fortunate coincidence 是句首状语，表示“巧合的是”。
be for 在此表示“需求的是、面向的是”，不是简单的“为了”。

sentences.sentence: Pilkington built a pilot plant in 1953 and by 1955 he had convinced his company to build a full-scale plant.
sentences.translation: 皮尔金顿于1953年建成一座试验工厂，到1955年，他已说服公司建造一座全规模工厂。

structure.text: he had convinced his company to build a full-scale plant

difficult_points: by 1955 常与过去完成时 had convinced 连用，表示到该过去时点前已完成。
convince sb. to do 表示“说服某人做某事”。

sentences.sentence: However, it took 14 months of non-stop production, costing the company £100,000 a month, before the plant produced any usable glass.
sentences.translation: 然而，工厂经历了14个月不间断生产、每月耗费公司10万英镑后，才生产出可用的玻璃。

structure.text: before the plant produced any usable glass

difficult_points: it took... before... 表示“花了……才……”，it 为形式主语。
costing... 是现在分词短语，补充说明这段生产造成的成本。

sentences.sentence: Furthermore, once they succeeded in making marketable flat glass, the machine was turned off for a service to prepare it for years of continuous production.
sentences.translation: 此外，一旦他们成功制出可销售的平板玻璃，机器就会停机检修，以便为多年连续生产做好准备。

structure.text: once they succeeded in making marketable flat glass
to prepare it for years of continuous production

difficult_points: succeed in 后接动名词 making，表示“成功做到”。
to prepare... 表示停机检修的目的，it 指 the machine。

sentences.sentence: When it started up again it took another four months to get the process right again.
sentences.translation: 机器重新启动后，又花了四个月才把工艺调整正确。

structure.text: to get the process right again

difficult_points: it 指前一句的 machine；start up 表示机器启动。
get the process right 表示把工艺调到正确状态，不是逐字“得到正确”。

sentences.sentence: They finally succeeded in 1959 and there are now float plants all over the world, with each able to produce around 1000 tons of glass every day, non-stop for around 15 years.
sentences.translation: 他们终于在1959年取得成功；如今世界各地都有浮法玻璃厂，每座都能每天生产约1000吨玻璃，并连续运转约15年。

structure.text: there are now float plants all over the world
with each able to produce around 1000 tons of glass every day, non-stop for around 15 years

difficult_points: succeed 在这里不带宾语，表示“取得成功”。
with each able to produce... 是 with 复合结构，each 指每一座工厂。

sentences.sentence: Float plants today make glass of near optical quality.
sentences.translation: 如今的浮法玻璃厂能制造接近光学级品质的玻璃。
difficult_points: of near optical quality 后置修饰 glass，说明品质等级。
near 在此表示“接近”，并非地点上的“附近”。

sentences.sentence: Several processes - melting, refining, homogenising - take place simultaneously in the 2000 tonnes of molten glass in the furnace.
sentences.translation: 熔化、精炼和均质化等几道工序，会在炉内2000吨熔融玻璃中同时进行。

structure.text: in the 2000 tonnes of molten glass in the furnace

difficult_points: 破折号中的三个动名词是 Several processes 的同位解释。
take place 表示“发生、进行”，不能按字面译为“占据地点”。

sentences.sentence: They occur in separate zones in a complex glass flow driven by high temperatures.
sentences.translation: 这些工序发生在不同区域，位于由高温驱动的复杂玻璃流动过程中。

structure.text: in a complex glass flow driven by high temperatures

difficult_points: They 指前一句的 Several processes。
driven by high temperatures 是过去分词短语，修饰 a complex glass flow。

sentences.sentence: It adds up to a continuous melting process, lasting as long as 50 hours, that delivers glass smoothly and continuously to the float bath, and from there to a coating zone and finally a heat treatment zone, where stresses formed during cooling are relieved.
sentences.translation: 这些步骤合起来构成一个连续熔化过程，持续长达50小时；该过程将玻璃平稳、连续地送入浮锡槽，再由那里送往镀膜区，最后到热处理区，在那里冷却过程中形成的应力会被消除。

structure.text: that delivers glass smoothly and continuously to the float bath, and from there to a coating zone and finally a heat treatment zone
where stresses formed during cooling are relieved

difficult_points: It 指前文的工序；add up to 表示“合计构成”。
that 修饰 process；where 修饰 heat treatment zone，formed during cooling 修饰 stresses。

sentences.sentence: The principle of float glass is unchanged since the 1950s.
sentences.translation: 浮法玻璃的基本原理自20世纪50年代以来一直没有改变。
difficult_points: is unchanged 表示一直保持未变的状态。
since the 1950s 指从过去持续至今的时间段。

sentences.sentence: However, the product has changed dramatically, from a single thickness of 6.8 mm to a range from sub-millimetre to 25 mm, from a ribbon frequently marred by inclusions and bubbles to almost optical perfection.
sentences.translation: 然而，产品已发生巨大变化：厚度从单一的6.8毫米变为从不足一毫米到25毫米不等；质量则从常被杂质和气泡损坏的玻璃带，变得几乎完美无瑕。

structure.text: from a single thickness of 6.8 mm to a range from sub-millimetre to 25 mm
from a ribbon frequently marred by inclusions and bubbles to almost optical perfection

difficult_points: has changed 表示从过去到现在已经发生的变化。
两个 from...to... 并列，分别说明厚度范围和品质的变化；marred... 修饰 ribbon。

sentences.sentence: To ensure the highest quality, inspection takes place at every stage.
sentences.translation: 为确保最高质量，每个阶段都会进行检测。
difficult_points: To ensure... 是不定式作目的状语。
takes place 表示“进行”，inspection 是不可数名词。

sentences.sentence: Occasionally, a bubble is not removed during refining, a sand grain refuses to melt, a tremor in the tin puts ripples into the glass ribbon.
sentences.translation: 有时，精炼过程中一个气泡没有被去除；一粒沙子不肯熔化；锡液中的一次颤动会使玻璃带产生波纹。

structure.text: a bubble is not removed during refining
a sand grain refuses to melt
a tremor in the tin puts ripples into the glass ribbon

difficult_points: 三个逗号分隔的分句并列列出不同缺陷来源。
refuses to melt 是拟人化说法，表示沙粒始终不熔化。

sentences.sentence: Automated on-line inspection does two things.
sentences.translation: 自动在线检测发挥两项作用。
difficult_points: does two things 不是字面“做两件事”，而是引出两项功能。
Automated 和 on-line 都前置修饰 inspection。

sentences.sentence: Firstly, it reveals process faults upstream that can be corrected.
sentences.translation: 第一，它能发现上游工序中可被修正的故障。
difficult_points: it 指 Automated on-line inspection。
that 修饰 process faults，can be corrected 表示这些故障可以被修正。

sentences.sentence: Inspection technology allows more than 100 million measurements a second to be made across the ribbon, locating flaws the unaided eye would be unable to see.
sentences.translation: 检测技术使人们能每秒在整条玻璃带上进行超过一亿次测量，从而定位肉眼无法看见的缺陷。

structure.text: more than 100 million measurements a second to be made across the ribbon
locating flaws the unaided eye would be unable to see

difficult_points: allow + 名词 + to be done 表示使某事得以被完成。
locating... 表示测量带来的结果；省略了 that 的从句修饰 flaws。

sentences.sentence: Secondly, it enables computers downstream to steer cutters around flaws.
sentences.translation: 第二，它使下游计算机能够引导切割器绕开缺陷。
difficult_points: enable sb. to do 表示“使某人或某物能够做……”。
downstream 后置修饰 computers；around flaws 表示“绕开缺陷”。

sentences.sentence: Float glass is sold by the square metre, and at the final stage computers translate customer requirements into patterns of cuts designed to minimise waste.
sentences.translation: 浮法玻璃按平方米销售；在最后阶段，计算机会把客户要求转化为旨在减少浪费的切割方案。

structure.text: computers translate customer requirements into patterns of cuts

difficult_points: by the square metre 表示按平方米计价或销售。
designed... 是过去分词短语，修饰 patterns of cuts，说明这些方案的目的。
